displayTitle: Pick the System That Fits the Job
kicker: PT LEVEL II // SELECTING THE METHOD

beats.narration: Every qualified penetrant will find a flaw. The Level Two skill is choosing the system whose advantages match the job.

payload.title: SELECTING THE METHOD
payload.sub: Match the system's advantages to the part.

beats.narration: Three picks build a system. Type: fluorescent for sensitivity, visible for daylight. Then a removal method. And, for fluorescent only, a sensitivity level.
Each removal method wins somewhere. Water-washable buys speed and rough castings. Postemulsifiable protects shallow flaws. Solvent-removable travels to the part in cans.
Then the sensitivity level. Match it to the smallest flaw the requirement names, not the biggest number on the shelf.
Type, method, level. Pick the smallest system that reliably reveals the flaw. Sensitivity beyond the requirement buys background, not safety.

payload.sub: Choosing the system is part of the examination.
